qa_pairs.question: Who is eligible to submit an application for the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC)?
qa_pairs.answer: To be eligible for the RAC, Principal Investigators (PIs) and Co-PIs must be faculty members at a Canadian academic institution and hold an active Alliance account with an Academic Principal Investigator role (such as Faculty, Adjunct Faculty, or Librarian). They must apply with their primary, most up-to-date position.

qa_pairs.question: What is the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) process?
qa_pairs.answer: The RAC involves two annual review processes: a peer-review by over 90 discipline-specific Canadian academic experts to assess project merits, and a technical review by Federation staff to verify resource accuracy and recommend national system allocation. The Resource Access Program Administrative Committee oversees the entire process.

qa_pairs.question: What types of resources are available to researchers through the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC)?
qa_pairs.answer: Through the RAC, researchers can request access to general-purpose systems, large shared memory systems, general-purpose computing on graphics processing units, storage, and cloud resources. The competition also provides technical support and training.
